meta.subtitle: Plain-English market read · Gap-risk check · Global chip selloff hits Wall Street · PCE Thursday
meta.rerun_note: Core PCE inflation report Thu 6/25 8:30 AM · re-run after · Micron earnings tonight

verdict.label: YELLOW · Trade small or sit out
verdict.desc: A global chip selloff is hitting the tape today: Asian and European semiconductors cratered — South Korea's Kospi slid sharply and Micron, Qualcomm and Arm are all down 8%+ in the U.S. as investors dump AI/chip names. The Nasdaq-100 is off nearly 3% and the S&P 500 is lower. Oil keeps sliding toward $73–74 on Iran peace progress, and volatility is creeping up (VIX ~17.3). On top of that, Micron reports tomorrow and Thursday's Core PCE inflation report is the week's big test. Take one small, well-supported trade at most, or wait for the inflation read.

composite.band_note: MILD · up from 41 · global chip rout + PCE ahead

gap.band_note: Below the danger line · rising on the chip rout; the bigger risk is Thursday's PCE

focus.jtag: Asia chips crash, U.S. semis follow; oil falls; PCE lands Thursday
focus.jnote: Today is a jittery "wait for the data" day. The big story is in chips: Asian and European semiconductors got hammered overnight and U.S. names followed — Micron fell about 11%, Qualcomm and Arm dropped roughly 8%, and the chip ETF SMH slid more than 6%. That selloff is dragging the Nasdaq lower while the Dow holds up better. Oil keeps sliding toward $73–74 on the U.S.–Iran 60-day roadmap and a 60-day license letting Iran sell oil, but last week's hawkish Fed still hangs over everything — traders now price a possible September rate hike (~68% odds) with the 10-year near 4.5%. Micron reports after the close tomorrow and Core PCE inflation lands Thursday, so size down.
focus.notdown: Industrials and financials held the Dow firmer than the broad tape, but that's rotation, not a single named catalyst, and a global risk-off wave could reverse it fast. Low grade.
focus.notup: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) stays the cleaner bearish read: oil is sliding toward $73–74 on the Iran 60-day roadmap, the new sales license, and Gulf producers ramping output. Real, durable news. Semis (SMH, NVDA, AVGO, MU) are dropping hard on the global chip rout — but shorting a fast-falling group on pure momentum into Micron earnings is risky. Energy half size; semis watch only.
focus.why: Why YELLOW and not GREEN? The gap-risk numbers (47 and 28%) are still calm enough for a green light on paper — but three rules apply: respect a fresh policy shock (the Fed just flipped from cuts to possible hikes), don't short a fast-falling market on pure momentum, and don't go full size right before a major inflation print. Core PCE lands Thursday 8:30 AM and Micron reports tomorrow night. Take one small, well-supported trade if it checks out at 9:35 AM, or wait. A cleaner read returns after PCE.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~17.3, calm but ticking up on the chip rout
Big events (next 1–3 days) — Core PCE Thursday + Micron earnings tomorrow night + flash PMIs
World risk — global chip selloff; Iran roadmap calming, but no signed deal yet
Volatility trend — ticking back up after easing post-Fed (estimated)
Commodities — oil down ~2.4%, which is calming for inflation, not scary

snapshot.delta: 4th down day · below IPO open · ineligible

setups.read: Best idea. Oil is sliding toward $73–74 on the Iran 60-day roadmap, the new 60-day oil-sales license, Hormuz traffic recovering, and Gulf producers ramping output — that caps the whole group. Real, durable news, but it's pre-PCE, so trade half size.

Falling hard on a global chip rout (Micron −11%, Qualcomm/Arm −8%+, SMH −6%+) after a historic run, but that's momentum, not a clean U.S. catalyst. Micron (MU) reports Wednesday night — earnings risk inside the window. Don't short into it. Wait.
Holding the Dow firmer than the broad tape (Caterpillar led Monday +~4%), but it's broad rotation with no single news story, and a global risk-off wave plus a hawkish Fed could reverse it fast. Wait.

Reports AMC Wednesday — earnings risk is in front of it, not behind it, and the stock is already down ~11% today on the chip rout. A spread now would straddle the print. Can't use it until the gap is set; revisit Thursday as a possible Type A.
Newly public, ultra-volatile (down a 4th straight day toward ~$150, below its IPO open, after a $20B+ bond sale): no real options history, no stable support, wild swings. Can't use it.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Choppy, tech-heavy digestion ahead of PCE
scenarios.sub: Chip selloff weighs on the Nasdaq; Dow steadier; everyone waits for Thursday's inflation read

scenarios.label: Bad case — Chip rout deepens, tech rolls over
scenarios.sub: The global semi selloff feeds U.S. tech and hike fears stick → broad risk-off, Nasdaq leads down

scenarios.label: Good case — Dip-buyers step in, oil keeps falling
scenarios.sub: Chip washout is treated as healthy; cheaper oil eases inflation worry → relief bounce

scenarios.label: Surprise — Iran stumbles or a shock hits
scenarios.sub: The 60-day roadmap breaks down, Micron shocks, or a hot data point lands → oil and volatility jump

risks: Core PCE inflation · Thu 6/25 8:30 AM — the Fed's favorite inflation gauge; with the Fed turning hawkish, a hot print could push yields and 2026 hike odds higher.

Global chip selloff — Asian and European semis crashed and U.S. names followed (Micron −11%, Qualcomm/Arm −8%+, SMH −6%+); that overheated-AI fear is bleeding into the whole group. Don't short on momentum alone.
Micron (MU) earnings Wednesday night — adds gap risk to the whole memory/semi complex inside a spread's window; the stock is already down sharply today.
Hike fears · short end firm — markets now price a possible September Fed hike (~68% odds) with the 10-year near 4.5%; growth and tech can drop fast if yields keep climbing.
Iran deal not signed yet — it's a 60-day roadmap, and tensions over Lebanon and Hormuz persist; oil could whipsaw on any setback over the long weekend.

rows.today: Risk-off: tech-led drop, chip rout global
Global chip selloff; Micron tomorrow; PCE Thu
